title: omphalea diandra
intro: Omphalea diandra, also known as the Philippine grape or velvet apple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of the Philippines and is highly valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant's extracts and essential oils have been found to have numerous health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-diabetic, and anti-cancer properties, as well as being used to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. Traditional herbal preparations of Omphalea diandra include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to make teas, syrups, and topical creams for various health applications.
